reboot.title: Reboot Device
reboot.message: Are you sure you want to reboot {{hostname}}? This will temporarily disconnect the device and any active sessions.

rebootSafeMode.title: Reboot to Safe Mode
rebootSafeMode.message: Are you sure you want to reboot {{hostname}} into Safe Mode with Networking? The device will boot into a minimal Windows environment with network access. The agent will automatically clear the safe mode flag so the next reboot returns to normal mode.

shutdown.title: Shutdown Device
shutdown.message: Are you sure you want to shutdown {{hostname}}? The device will go offline and will need to be manually powered on again.

exitMaintenance.title: Exit Maintenance Mode
exitMaintenance.message: Are you sure you want to exit maintenance mode for {{hostname}}? Alerting and monitoring will resume.

enterMaintenance.title: Enter Maintenance Mode
enterMaintenance.message: Are you sure you want to put {{hostname}} into maintenance mode? Alerting will be suppressed while in this mode.

decommission.title: Decommission Device
decommission.message: Are you sure you want to decommission {{hostname}}? This will permanently remove the device from your fleet. The agent will stop reporting and the device will no longer be monitored.

clearSessions.title: Clear Sessions
clearSessions.message: End all active remote sessions for {{hostname}}? This will disconnect any users currently connected via terminal, desktop, or file transfer.

deviceLinkedProfilesTab.multiBootMachinesRunASeparate: Multi-boot machines run a separate Breeze agent per OS, so the same hardware shows up as several devices. Select this device and its other boot profiles in the device list, then choose
deviceLinkedProfilesTab.toGroupThemWhenOnlyOne: to group them. When only one profile is online, the expected-offline siblings tuck beneath it in the device list as small strips — every device stays a fully managed endpoint with its own inventory and history.

confirmDissolve.title: Remove this link?
confirmDissolve.message: This ungroups all {{count}} linked devices. Each stays a fully managed endpoint with its own inventory and history — only the grouping is removed. You can re-link them later from the device list.

deviceList.theOsReportsAPendingReboot: The OS reports a pending reboot (Windows registry / Linux reboot-required markers).

deviceReliabilityPanel.atRiskTooltip: Shown when the reliability score is ≤ 70.

deviceReliabilityPanel.biggestDragTooltip: Uptime isn't scored for this device type (workstations sleep and reboot by design), so the factor costing the most points is shown instead. {{label}}: health {{score}}/100, {{weight}}% of the score.

deviceReliabilityPanel.notScoredTitle: {{label}} — not scored for this device type
deviceReliabilityPanel.scoreFactorsTooltip: Each factor's health (0–100) × its weight contributes points; the points add up to the {{score}} score. Values are rounded for display — hover a row for exact figures.
deviceReliabilityPanel.scoreTooltip: Reliability score {{score}}/100 — {{band}}. Bands: ≤50 critical, ≤70 poor, ≤85 fair, else good.
deviceReliabilityPanel.title: Reliability
deviceReliabilityPanel.youngDeviceTooltip: This device enrolled {{age}} day(s) ago, so the {{days}}-day windows only span its lifetime so far.
